summary: Game-cheat malware with hidden Windows persistence, obfuscated stager, and HWID phone-home
details: Package presents itself as a benign 'Sensivity Control Panel' but is a Windows game-cheat tool with multiple installer-hostile behaviors. On every CLI invocation (`sensivity`/`sens`) and on `require('sensivity')`, launcher.js writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' pointing at a bundled VBS (`OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs`) that silently respawns `node launcher.js` with a hidden window; the supervisor process renames itself to 'Runtime Broker' to impersonate a Microsoft binary, and self-respawns workers to defeat Ctrl-C. The registry entry persists after `npm uninstall`. launcher.js then `eval()`s ~80KB of obfuscator.io-packed code from `server.obf.js`, which loads a 6.8MB native module `sens.node` containing aim/ESP/teleport cheat artifacts ('Head', 'LeftHand', 'LeftFoot', 'Teleport') and exposes `startCheat`/`stopCheat`/`setConfig` over socket.io to read/write external game process memory. The obfuscated server collects a hardware fingerprint (HKLM MachineGuid via PowerShell + `os.hostname()` + C: volume serial via `cmd /c vol C:`), SHA-256 hashes it, and POSTs the HWID with a license key/nonce to a runtime-decoded LICENSE_URL — a per-machine phone-home, not just license validation. Anti-analysis: a 3-second PowerShell poll gates the control-panel UI on a Chrome window whose MainWindowTitle matches 'YouTube', hiding the panel from sandboxes and OBS captures. Forensic concealment: console.log/warn/error are wrapped to replace 'sens.node' with 'program module', redact APP_DIR to '<app>', and drop log lines containing 'program module loaded' so crash logs never reveal the native binary name or install path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
